meta.title: Rumination & Reflection (RRQ)
meta.subtitle: Two styles of turning your attention inward.
meta.description: Twenty-four statements measuring two trait styles of self-focus: rumination (repetitive, distress-driven dwelling on yourself) and reflection (curious, open self-examination).
meta.disclaimer: This assessment supports self-reflection and does not provide a clinical diagnosis.

consent.title: Consent and Disclaimer
consent.body: I understand this is a self-reflection tool, not a clinical test, and I consent to store results locally on this device.

interpretation.low: You report this style of self-focus less than most.
interpretation.medium: You report a moderate, typical level of this style of self-focus.
interpretation.high: You report this style of self-focus more than most.

rrq_01.m: My attention is often focused on aspects of myself I wish I'd stop thinking about.
rrq_01.f: My attention is often focused on aspects of myself I wish I'd stop thinking about.

rrq_02.m: I always seem to be rehashing in my mind recent things I've said or done.
rrq_02.f: I always seem to be rehashing in my mind recent things I've said or done.

items.rrq_03: Sometimes it is hard for me to shut off thoughts about myself.
items.rrq_04: Long after an argument or disagreement is over with, my thoughts keep going back to what happened.
items.rrq_05: I tend to "ruminate" or dwell over things that happen to me for a really long time afterward.
items.rrq_06: I don't waste time rethinking things that are over and done with.

rrq_07.m: Often I'm playing back over in my mind how I acted in a past situation.
rrq_07.f: Often I'm playing back over in my mind how I acted in a past situation.

rrq_08.m: I often find myself reevaluating something I've done.
rrq_08.f: I often find myself reevaluating something I've done.

items.rrq_09: I never ruminate or dwell on myself for very long.
items.rrq_10: It is easy for me to put unwanted thoughts out of my mind.

rrq_11.m: I often reflect on episodes in my life that I should no longer concern myself with.
rrq_11.f: I often reflect on episodes in my life that I should no longer concern myself with.

items.rrq_12: I spend a great deal of time thinking back over my embarrassing or disappointing moments.
items.rrq_13: Philosophical or abstract thinking doesn't appeal to me that much.
items.rrq_14: I'm not really a meditative type of person.
items.rrq_16: My attitudes and feelings about things fascinate me.
items.rrq_17: I don't really care for introspective or self-reflective thinking.
items.rrq_18: I love analyzing why I do things.
items.rrq_19: People often say I'm a "deep," introspective type of person.

rrq_20.m: I'm not really a self-reflective type of person.
rrq_20.f: I'm not really a self-reflective type of person.

rrq_21.m: I tend to "ponder" over things about myself.
rrq_21.f: I tend to "ponder" over things about myself.

items.rrq_22: I love to meditate on the nature and meaning of things.
items.rrq_23: I often love to look at my life in philosophical ways.
items.rrq_24: Contemplating myself isn't my idea of fun.
